description_long: This object is a classic regulatory stop sign, featuring a red octagonal face with a bold white border and the word 'STOP' printed in large, uppercase white letters at the center. The sign is mounted on a vertical, metallic-looking post that extends below the sign face, typical of those found at road intersections. The red face of the sign is likely made from a reflective material to ensure high visibility both day and night. The post appears to be constructed from galvanized steel or a similar durable metal, providing stability and resistance to weather. The rear of the sign is plain and gray, lacking any text or markings, and the sign face is slightly thicker than the post. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing visibility, durability, and clear communication to drivers and pedestrians.
description: A red octagonal stop sign with white uppercase 'STOP' text, mounted on a metallic post. The sign is highly visible and designed for roadside use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the red octagonal sign face with white 'STOP' text and a metallic post.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thin profile of the sign and post, with the sign face slightly protruding from the post.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the plain gray back of the octagonal sign and the metallic post, with no visible text.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the thin profile, similar to view 1, with the sign face and post aligned.
